name: GitHub Issue to Agent Dispatch
purpose: Claim Codex-ready GitHub issues created from Plane and dispatch them through OpenClaw to Codex or Hermes.
eligibility: Labels include plane, codex-ready, and automation.
Labels do not include codex-in-progress, hermes-in-progress, codex-pr-open, done, or blocked.
Issue body includes plane_issue_id and plane_project_id.
Issue is not a pull request.
core_logic: Parse Plane metadata and runner from the GitHub issue body.
Resolve runner auto to codex unless the issue explicitly asks for hermes.
Claim the GitHub issue with an in-progress label.
Dispatch OpenClaw with repo, GitHub issue, Plane issue, Plane project, runner, and callback URL.
Resolve the Plane Building state by name within the issue project.
Move the Plane item to Building only after dispatch is accepted.
Ignore duplicate or already-claimed webhook deliveries.
safe_failure_handling: Do not dispatch if required metadata is missing.
Do not dispatch if the issue is already claimed or has an open PR label.
Use GitHub labels as visible claim state.
Use Plane project ID from issue metadata, not a hardcoded project ID.
Routine dispatch should not notify Slack.
